jokes.text: These fresh meatballs in here? Gotta get that corn on the cob. On the cob, off the cob, it don't matter. Y'all ain't playing around this motherfucker, huh?
jokes.setup: Leon excitedly surveys the buffet
jokes.punchline: His casual profanity mixed with buffet enthusiasm - 'on the cob, off the cob, it don't matter' treating corn preparation like profound philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon - his street philosophy applied to mundane buffet choices, with perfect character voice

jokes.text: Leon's urination time-wasting theory leading to 'The Peeus' car concept
jokes.setup: Leon asks Larry about time wasted urinating
jokes.punchline: Elaborate car that runs on urine called 'The Peeus'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation from reasonable observation to completely insane business idea. 'The Peeus' is quotable perfection.

jokes.text: You know who's gonna be a billionaire? Motherfucker who's gonna, uh, create a car that runs on piss... The Peeus.
jokes.setup: Leon's bathroom time efficiency theory
jokes.punchline: Elaborate piss-powered car concept culminating in 'The Peeus' - brilliant pun on Prius
jokes.explanation: Masterful Leon invention - the detailed logistics of dick-to-gas-tank mechanics plus genius 'Peeus' wordplay. Peak absurdist character comedy

jokes.text: Leon's pre-eating preparation: undoing pants and belt buckle
jokes.setup: Leon announcing he's getting more food
jokes.punchline: Physical preparation like he's going to war
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon excess - treating eating like a military operation

jokes.text: Shimon's itemized accounting: 'Five pound short rib, ten pound prawns'
jokes.setup: Restaurant owner trying to justify banning Leon
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific weight measurements of Leon's consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity makes the absurd consumption concrete and funnier

jokes.text: Oh, so you're the Wendy's of this motherfucker. I'm fucking Wendy. You want me to put on a little red wig and freckles?
jokes.setup: Restaurant owner Shimon introduces himself as authority figure
jokes.punchline: Leon immediately compares him to Wendy's mascot and offers to roleplay as the pigtailed girl
jokes.explanation: Leon's mind works in mysterious ways - the leap from restaurant owner to fast food mascot to cross-dressing roleplay is pure Leon logic

jokes.text: The escalating 'You stink!' exchange between Larry and Shimon
jokes.setup: Larry trying to defend Leon leads to argument
jokes.punchline: Devolution into childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation but saved by how it fits Larry's tendency to get drawn into petty arguments

jokes.text: Only person besides you, Gary Busey.
jokes.setup: Shimon explaining Leon's lifetime ban
jokes.punchline: Reveals Leon is only the second person ever banned, alongside notoriously erratic Gary Busey
jokes.explanation: Perfect celebrity name-drop - puts Leon in exclusive company with Hollywood's most unpredictable actor. Economical and perfectly chosen reference

jokes.text: If this was a brothel, I'd be fucking pissed.
jokes.setup: Leon complaining about the lifetime ban
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'fucking pissed' in the context of a hypothetical brothel
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay from Leon - the pun works on multiple levels and shows his twisted logic applying restaurant bans to sex work

jokes.text: She loves to play golf, and I was just hoping that maybe you could sponsor her at the club... Because of that whole college admissions scandal thing, no club will touch her.
jokes.setup: Ted casually asking Larry to sponsor Lori Loughlin
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's been blackballed from golf clubs due to her criminal conviction
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference - the scandal's real-world consequences played for comedy, though not particularly original

jokes.text: But you did the finale, right? - Yeah, Ted, I did the finale.
jokes.setup: Ted asking about Larry's Seinfeld involvement
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperated tone suggests this is asked constantly - meta-joke about Larry David's real career
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Larry's fame - his weary response suggests he gets this question constantly. Character-specific but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Catch as Caftan
jokes.setup: Susie revealing her caftan business name
jokes.punchline: Play on 'catch as catch can' - the business name is a forced pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun for a business name but feels forced - the kind of wordplay that's mildly clever but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Sienna Miller - She's going out with you? - Yeah! - That gorgeous woman?
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals his date with Sienna Miller
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction emphasizes how unlikely this pairing is
jokes.explanation: Standard 'unlikely couple' reaction comedy - works for character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Larry's shocked reaction: 'She's going out with you?' and 'That gorgeous woman?'
jokes.setup: Jeff revealing he's dating Sienna Miller
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about the mismatch
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - saying what everyone's thinking with zero filter

jokes.text: By the way, if you think you're getting any of my popcorn, you're gravely mistaken. Gravely.
jokes.setup: Larry and Sienna at movie theater, Larry reciting Gettysburg Address
jokes.punchline: 'Gravely' used both as intensifier and Lincoln/cemetery pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting Larry's petty popcorn hoarding to Lincoln's grave subject matter - good character integration

jokes.text: I'm like Rachel and the rug merchants... It's a famous Old Testament Jewish parable... Call yourself a Jew?
jokes.setup: Larry claims to be busy like a Biblical reference
jokes.punchline: Makes up fake Jewish parable then shames Sienna for not knowing it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry move - inventing religious authority to make a point, then using that fake authority to criticize others. Peak Larry logic

jokes.text: Did she have an affair with the rug merchant? What happened with her and the rug merchant?
jokes.setup: Sienna trying to understand Larry's fake parable
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly specific and dramatic interpretations of the non-existent story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Sienna fills in Larry's fake story with soap opera drama - shows how his BS spreads

jokes.text: Sienna's increasingly specific questions about the rug merchant story
jokes.setup: Larry unable to explain his made-up parable
jokes.punchline: Each question reveals how hollow his reference was
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry getting deeper into his lie through Sienna's logical questioning

jokes.text: I notice you like to eat a lot of fruit in your movies... Is that part of your process? You take a bite, you act, you take another bite, you act.
jokes.setup: Larry making small talk during movie
jokes.punchline: His bizarrely specific observation about Sienna's fruit consumption patterns in films
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry observation - the kind of weird detail only he would notice and comment on. Sets up major story thread

jokes.text: Like you're giving me a lap dance, okay? - Oh, please. Everybody knows that a jacket goes on the back of the chair.
jokes.setup: Argument over Larry's jacket placement
jokes.punchline: Escalates from minor etiquette to sexual imagery while child still missing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate escalation - turning jacket etiquette into sexual metaphor shows his oblivious priorities

jokes.text: I can't just go to the mall and fuck any woman I want.
jokes.setup: Coat argument continuing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about mall sexual freedom in response to jacket placement
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation - the logic connecting marriage fidelity to jacket placement is completely insane but delivered seriously

jokes.text: Eighty-seven years ago, our fathers brought forth on this property a new golf club dedicated to the proposition that men can have fun and women can't.
jokes.setup: Larry giving speech to golf club board using Gettysburg Address format
jokes.punchline: Applies Lincoln's equality language to defend golf club's historical sexism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - using Lincoln's equality speech to defend inequality. Perfect Larry obliviousness to irony

jokes.text: Larry's parody conclusion: 'this exclusive club of specific people, by specific people, and for specific people shall have a new, specific member'
jokes.setup: Continuing the Gettysburg Address parody
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'the people' with 'specific people' to acknowledge exclusivity
jokes.explanation: Clever word substitution that maintains the rhythm while exposing the hypocrisy

jokes.text: And as much as that woman in Atlanta was thirsty for water, Lori Loughlin is equally thirsty for membership! I say we let her drink!
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his Gettysburg parody speech
jokes.punchline: Connecting Lori Loughlin's desire for golf membership to civil rights imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the speech parody - Larry's tone-deaf comparison of golf membership to civil rights struggle

jokes.text: You know, people say that, and... and they don't really. They stop thanking. You'll... Probably won't thank me again after this.
jokes.setup: Lori Loughlin thanking Larry profusely
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical prediction that gratitude is temporary
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pessimism - his dark view of human nature applied to simple gratitude. Very character-specific

jokes.text: There's a dick in my mouth? - In your mouth. - With balls? - A drawing. A drawing.
jokes.setup: Susie discovering vandalism on her billboard
jokes.punchline: The increasingly detailed revelation of the graffiti's anatomical completeness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - each question reveals more disturbing details. The repetition builds the cringe effectively

jokes.text: You're cradling the balls.
jokes.setup: Description of penis graffiti continues
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact delivery of the most embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the pause before Larry delivers the worst news with clinical detachment

jokes.text: Why can't... why can't you give a blow job every now and then? Would that kill ya?
jokes.setup: Jeff insisting he doesn't give oral sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's persistent encouragement like he's suggesting Jeff try a new hobby
jokes.explanation: Larry's casual tone about intimate acts - treating sexual acts like lifestyle choices to be optimized

jokes.text: Larry's argument about shame: 'Why is there gonna be shame on number six?'
jokes.setup: Continuing the masturbation debate
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical consistency applied to inappropriate topic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - applying rigorous logic to completely inappropriate analogy

jokes.text: Shimon's final dismissal: 'Shalom, bitch!'
jokes.setup: Escalating argument with Larry
jokes.punchline: Mixing Hebrew greeting with modern insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect two-word comedy - cultural mixing that's both offensive and hilarious. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: Rachel. She looks at the rug merchants and says, 'You're banned, bitch!' Shalom!
jokes.setup: Israeli man asked about Rachel and rug merchants story
jokes.punchline: Makes up his own version incorporating current situation with Shimon's 'banned' language
jokes.explanation: Great callback structure - the fake parable gets corrupted through retelling, becoming about the current situation

jokes.text: The jogger's version: 'Rachel... looks at the rug merchants and says, "You're banned, bitch!" Shalom!'
jokes.setup: Larry asking about Rachel and rug merchants
jokes.punchline: Jogger making up story incorporating recent 'Shalom, bitch' moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback combining two running gags into one perfect moment

jokes.text: Lori's Epstein-Barr excuse for handicapped parking
jokes.setup: Lori explaining her handicapped golf cart privileges
jokes.punchline: Dubious medical diagnosis from one doctor
jokes.explanation: Good setup for character flaws - Lori gaming the system like the college scandal

jokes.text: Isn't that a Jew disease?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Epstein-Barr syndrome
jokes.punchline: Casual anti-Semitic assumption about disease patterns
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment - casual prejudice delivered matter-of-factly creates discomfort but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Yeah! Fuck you, Shimon! That's five pounds of short ribs and ten pounds of fuck you!
jokes.setup: Leon celebrating at Lincoln play performance
jokes.punchline: Turns theater triumph into restaurant revenge fantasy using Shimon's earlier measurements
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff - Leon weaponizes Shimon's precise measurements into the perfect insult. Pure character gold

jokes.text: Susie's business boom revelation and Larry's 'post-penis bump' theory
jokes.setup: Susie's business suddenly thriving
jokes.punchline: Larry's theory that the penis graffiti is driving sales
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - the vandalism actually helps business. 'Post-penis bump' is quotable.

jokes.text: You need a second dick... Dick up the ass... And how 'bout some jizz on ya?
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting Susie embrace the penis marketing strategy
jokes.punchline: Escalates to increasingly graphic vandalism suggestions as business advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - Larry treats increasingly obscene vandalism as legitimate business strategy with entrepreneurial enthusiasm

jokes.text: I got you a pear.
jokes.setup: Larry's fruit theory for fixing Sienna's performance
jokes.punchline: Simple, matter-of-fact delivery of the solution
jokes.explanation: Beautiful deadpan delivery - the simple solution to a complex problem, delivered like he's offering basic hospitality

jokes.text: Larry bringing a pear to solve Sienna's acting problems
jokes.setup: Sienna struggling with her performance, claiming she doesn't like fruit
jokes.punchline: Larry's simple solution - bringing fruit to set
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier setup. Simple visual gag that shows Larry's problem-solving approach.

jokes.text: You know what else I tried to find was the word schmeitz, which... I couldn't find that either. - Schmeitz is a real word for 'I've got to go.' 'I got to schmeitz.'
jokes.setup: Sienna researching Larry's made-up Jewish references
jokes.punchline: Larry doubles down with another fake Yiddish word, defining it confidently
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Larry's fake expertise - caught in one lie, he immediately creates another with complete confidence

jokes.text: Larry's continued insistence on the fake 'schmeitz' word meaning 'I've got to go'
jokes.setup: Sienna saying she can't find the word 'schmeitz'
jokes.punchline: Larry doubling down on his made-up Yiddish
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Larry's fake cultural knowledge - he never admits when he's wrong

jokes.text: I have alopecia, you fucking asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about Sienna's ever-changing hair in movies
jokes.punchline: Reveals his innocent curiosity exposed her medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Larry's casual curiosity becomes deeply personal and embarrassing. Great dramatic timing

jokes.text: Don't schmeitz! Don't schmeitz!
jokes.setup: Sienna storming out after alopecia revelation
jokes.punchline: Larry desperately using his fake Yiddish word to try to stop her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback payoff - Larry's desperation leads him to use his own made-up word as if it will actually help the situation

jokes.text: I can think of 50 reasons.
jokes.setup: Larry asking how Lori got preferential tee time
jokes.punchline: Implies sexual favors with knowing delivery
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo - economical delivery but fairly predictable sexual implication

jokes.text: Yeah, you had a good lie all right.
jokes.setup: Lori claiming she found her ball for a good score
jokes.punchline: Larry's accusation using golf terminology double meaning
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay - 'lie' works as both golf ball position and dishonesty accusation. Economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: On the count of three, turn your head to the right. One, two, three... Oh, yeah. And keep my wife's business out of your motherfucking mouth!
jokes.setup: Fan wanting photo with 'penis billboard lady'
jokes.punchline: Will Smith slap parody with 'mouth' pun referencing the billboard
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference mashup - combines current events with show's running gag, though feels slightly forced

jokes.text: Coat argument continuation during Lincoln play: 'Coat long. And fish dead.'
jokes.setup: Same waiter from theater having coat dispute
jokes.punchline: Combining current argument with ongoing fish obsession
jokes.explanation: Good combination of running gags showing how Larry's conflicts follow him everywhere

jokes.text: I've been memorizing the Gettysburg Address every time I pee, and I'm conditioned to pee when I hear it... It's like a Pavlovian response.
jokes.setup: Larry needing bathroom during Lincoln's speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's accidentally conditioned himself through bathroom practice sessions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - connects Leon's bathroom efficiency theory with Larry's speech practice, creating perfect Pavlovian conditioning comedy

jokes.text: I didn't know she had alopecia!
jokes.setup: Larry's final desperate defense to police
jokes.punchline: Ends episode with his most embarrassing mistake as his last plea
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback - Larry's most cringe moment becomes his final desperate defense, showing he still doesn't understand why it was wrong
